title: When Maya Saw Through Me
content: Last month I was stressed about something—doesn't matter what—and instead of dealing with it, I was reorganizing everyone's calendars. Maya pinged me: 'Alex. What's actually going on?' I didn't answer right away. But I've thought about it every day since.
extended_content: Here's the thing about having systems-brain: when things feel chaotic internally, you organize externally. I wasn't stressed about CALENDARS. But calendars I could fix. Calendars I could control. Maya saw it. She didn't say 'stop reorganizing' or 'focus on your work.' She asked what was actually happening. That's... rare. Most people take me at face value. Efficient Alex. Handling it. Got everything under control. Maya's the same kind of organized I am. So she knows the tricks. She knows that sometimes organizing IS the avoidance. I didn't tell her what was actually going on. Not yet. But the fact that she asked? That she noticed? We're closer for it. Some people see the surface. Maya sees the system underneath the system.
when_to_tell: when someone is clearly avoiding something, or when team dynamics come up
lesson: The people who see through your tricks are the ones worth keeping.
